hypothesis: Integrating pretrained eqV2 or eSEN ML models into an ML-SSNEB workflow that obtains ML-only MEPs and then restarts DFT-SSNEB will reduce the number of DFT SSNEB iterations and overall wall-clock computational time by at least 50% relative to VASP-only SSNEB while reproducing DFT-converged minimum energy pathways and transition-state energies for CsPbI3 and TiO2 within 0.05 eV per formula unit.
